title: cynoglossum kuramense
intro: Cynoglossum kuramense, also known as the Kashmir hound's tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Himalayan region, and its roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in reducing fever, relieving rheumatism and arthritis, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant are typically dried and made into decoctions or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including reduced stress and improved immune function.
